declared_losses.description: Assumption that projector would work correctly on first 6 turns of real session without code inspection
declared_losses.context: Cycle 51 ended with 88-92% confidence in Blocker 3 success. This was based only on dependency verification (uv sync, smoke test file reading). Did not inspect projector.py parsing logic for edge cases, format assumptions, or robustness.
declared_losses.impact: Wasted 1 turn (Turn 155) on execution doomed to fail due to TypeError in strand parsing. Projector logic was untested before deployment.
declared_losses.recovery: Mandatory code inspection before next execution. Fix parsing to handle multiple format variants. Add defensive error handling and logging.

declared_losses.description: Over-reliance on smoke test (session file reading) as proxy for projector logic validation
declared_losses.context: Cycle 51 smoke test verified that session file could be read and parsed (55 turns extracted). This was interpreted as evidence that 'system is working'. However, smoke test did not invoke projector.project() or validate tensor output structure.
declared_losses.impact: Gave false confidence in integration readiness. Parsing logic was completely untested. Confidence estimate 88-92% was unjustified.
declared_losses.recovery: Higher bar for 'readiness' claims. Require minimal execution test (1-turn projector run) before declaring system ready. Smoke tests must include actual projector invocation, not just dependencies.

declared_losses.description: Assumption that Claude API responses would be structurally consistent across multiple calls within same session
declared_losses.context: Projector calls Claude API for each turn. Cycle 1 projection succeeded (4 strands, 15 claims printed). Cycle 2+ projections failed with TypeError during strand extraction. This suggests Claude returned different JSON structure in cycle 2.
declared_losses.impact: Projector has tight coupling between API response format and parsing logic. Single format variation breaks entire pipeline. This fragility was not anticipated.
declared_losses.recovery: Add defensive parsing that handles multiple strand formats (array of dicts, string-wrapped JSON, nested structures). Add logging to capture actual API responses. Consider stricter prompt engineering to enforce consistent structure.

instructions_for_next: CYCLE 52 EXECUTION SUMMARY & HANDOFF TO CYCLE 53: BLOCKER 3 FAILED AT RUNTIME (Turn 156). TypeError in projector.py line 171: 'string indices must be integers, not str'. Root cause: strand extraction logic expects list of dicts, but receives string (or malformed structure). Cycle 1 projection succeeded (4 strands generated, printed). Cycle 2+ failed during parsing. Likely cause: Claude API returned strands field in different format across turns. IMMEDIATE NEXT CYCLE (53) ACTIONS: (1) CODE INSPECTION (Turn 157): Read projector.py _parse_projection() lines 160-180. Document exact JSON structure assumptions. (2) OUTPUT INSPECTION (Turn 158): Check for any files in /tmp or experiments/first_projection. Look for cycle 1 successful response structure. (3) FIX DEVELOPMENT (Turn 159): Add defensive parsing for multiple strand formats. Handle: (a) strands as array of dicts, (b) strands as JSON string, (c) strands nested in alternate path. Add error logging. (4) RE-RUN TEST (Turn 160): Execute fixed code with same 6-turn test. uv run python -m hamutay.run_projection ... 6 2>&1 Expected: exit code 0, no TypeError. (5) VALIDATION (Turns 161-162): Check output structure, epistemic values, strands count. If validation passes, declare BLOCKER 3 = RESOLVED ✓ (caveat: 6-turn slice only). CONFIDENCE IN FIX: 75-80%. Parsing issue is isolatable. Defensive coding should resolve. If fix fails, deeper issue (API format, not parsing). BLOCKER 3 REVISED ESTIMATE: Success likelihood post-fix: 70% (was 88-92%). Additional turns: 8-12. Project timeline: +1-2 cycles (Cycle 53 likely needed). CRITICAL LEARNING: Small-slice testing (6 turns vs 55) was CORRECT STRATEGY. Caught logic error early without burning 49-turn execution waste. Phased testing is BEST PRACTICE. BACKUP PLAN: If fix fails, simplify projector to 1-turn execution and add verbose logging. Then gradually scale up. DO NOT ATTEMPT FULL 55-TURN RUN until 6-turn test passes. HANDOFF NOTE: Cycle 51 predictions were too high (88-92% → 0% actual). Cycle 52 analysis was more realistic (70-75% forward estimate). Confidence calibration improving. Continue tracking prediction accuracy through Cycle 53+.
